description: Review flashcards on integumentary system and cytogenetics

cards.text: Name the five layers of the epidermis from deep to superficial.
cards.answer: Stratum basale, stratum spinosum, stratum granulosum, stratum lucidum, stratum corneum/basale spinosum granulosum lucidum corneum

cards.text: What are the two layers of the dermis and their key features?
cards.answer: Papillary dermis: thin, loose connective tissue with Meissner corpuscles. Reticular dermis: thick, dense irregular connective tissue with Pacinian corpuscles/papillary (loose CT) and reticular (dense irregular CT)

tip.tip: Papillary = fine touch; reticular = deep pressure

cards.text: What is the difference between eccrine and apocrine sweat glands?
cards.answer: Eccrine: entire body, merocrine secretion, watery sweat, open to skin surface. Apocrine: axilla and groin, open into hair follicles, thick secretion, activated at puberty/eccrine are merocrine everywhere; apocrine in axilla open into follicles

tip.tip: Apocrine secretion becomes odorous after bacterial action

cards.text: What type of secretion do sebaceous glands use?
cards.answer: Holocrine secretion — the entire cell disintegrates to release sebum/holocrine

tip.tip: Sebaceous glands are part of the pilosebaceous unit

cards.text: In pemphigus vulgaris, at which epidermal level does the blister form?
cards.answer: Intraepidermal cleavage at stratum spinosum; autoantibodies attack desmosomes (desmoglein)/stratum spinosum desmosomes
The inactivated X chromosome visible as a condensed chromatin body at the nuclear periphery in female cells/inactive X chromosome condensed at nuclear periphery

tip.tip: Number of Barr bodies = number of X chromosomes minus 1

cards.text: Based on centromere position, how are chromosomes classified?
cards.answer: Metacentric (middle), submetacentric (off-centre), acrocentric (near end with satellites), telocentric (at end — not in humans)/metacentric submetacentric acrocentric telocentric
In every female somatic cell, one X chromosome is randomly inactivated early in embryonic development; the choice is random but permanent for all daughter cells/random X-inactivation in female cells

tip.tip: Explains variable expression in carrier females of X-linked disorders

cards.text: What staining technique is used for chromosome banding in karyotyping?
cards.answer: G-banding (Giemsa staining after trypsin digestion) is the most common technique/G-banding with Giemsa stain

tip.tip: Each chromosome has a unique banding pattern
